details: , implemented by the Tourism Department under the Chief Minister’s CM-ELEVATE Program of the Planning, Investment Promotion & Sustainable Development Department, Government of Meghalaya, aims to boost tourism in the state by providing 50% financial assistance for the purchase of adventure tourism vehicles. The scheme also offers easy credit through partner banks, training for operators, and government-funded GPS and branding support, thereby enabling entrepreneurs and entities to enhance tourist transport services and promote economic growth.
benefits: 50% of on-road vehicle cost supported (5% by the entrepreneur, 45% by the Government of Meghalaya)
Pre-fitted GPS device with 5-year warranty (cost borne by Government, one-time)
Branding cost of vehicle borne by the Government (first time only)
Mandatory capacity building and training programs under Meghalaya Tourist Operator Program (MTOTP) by the Tourism Department
Support from PRIME – Tourism Vehicle Cell for promotion, grievance redressal, and training
Eligibility for multiple vehicle types, including 8–17 seater luxury buses, 4x4 vehicles, and MPVs for tourism and adventure tourism
eligibility: The applicant should be between 18 and 58 years of age.
The applicant should be a permanent resident of Meghalaya.
The applicant should possess a valid commercial driving license.
The applicant should be involved in the tourism sector.
The applicant should be associated with the adventure tourism sector if applying for a 4x4 vehicle.
The applicant should not be a credit defaulter with any bank or financial institution.
The applicant should be a recognised and registered tour operator, tourism society, or similar entity based in Meghalaya.
The applicant should have experience in the tourism sector.
The applicant should not be a beneficiary under the first phase of the PRIME Tourism Vehicle Scheme.
The applicant should provide an Incorporation Certificate or Registration Certificate, as applicable.
The applicant should be an unregistered entity such as a village organization, self-help group, or traditional community based in Meghalaya.
The applicant should provide certification from the Block Development Officer (BDO) to support involvement in the tourism sector.

documents_required: Proof of Address of Applicant (Voter ID / Passport / Driving License) (if available)
Proof of Address of Applicant (Voter ID / Passport / Driving License) - President or Secretary
PAN Card of President or Secretary

faqs.question: What is the PRIME Tourism Vehicle Scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme is an initiative of the Government of Meghalaya to promote tourism by providing financial assistance for purchasing adventure tourism vehicles under the CM-ELEVATE Program.

faqs.question: What financial support is provided under the scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme provides 50% financial assistance for the purchase of approved adventure tourism vehicles.

faqs.question: Who can apply for the scheme?
faqs.answer: Individuals, registered entities, and eligible unregistered entities based in Meghalaya and involved in the tourism sector can apply.

faqs.question: What is the age limit for individual applicants?
faqs.answer: Individual applicants must be between 18 and 58 years of age.

faqs.question: Is a driving license mandatory for individuals?
faqs.answer: Yes, individual applicants must possess a valid commercial driving license.

faqs.question: Can applicants apply for 4x4 vehicles?
faqs.answer: Yes, but applicants applying for 4x4 vehicles must be associated with the adventure tourism sector.

faqs.question: Are credit defaulters eligible to apply?
faqs.answer: No, applicants must not be credit defaulters with any bank or financial institution.

faqs.question: Can registered tour operators apply?
faqs.answer: Yes, recognized and registered tour operators, tourism societies, and similar entities based in Meghalaya are eligible.

faqs.question: How can applicants submit their application?
faqs.answer: Applications must be submitted online through the Meghalaya One Portal along with all required supporting documents.

faqs.question: What is the selection process?
faqs.answer: Applications are screened by District and State Committees, followed by verification, and shortlisted candidates may undergo interviews and inspections.
